title: Abdominal pain
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

presenting_history.text: Specifies pain characteristics, systemic symptoms, diet, stool, vomiting, urinary, adolescent, psychosocial, and infant presentation history.
presenting_history.rationale: Directly supports mapped pain, systemic, stool, vomiting, urinary, diet, and infant-history domains.
presenting_history.evidence_extract: Specifies pain characteristics, systemic symptoms, diet, stool, vomiting, urinary, adolescent, psychosocial, and infant presentation history.

source.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Abdominal pain — acute

red_flags.text: Defines acute pediatric abdominal pain as a common nonspecific presentation requiring assessment for urgent causes and red flags.
red_flags.rationale: Directly supports acute pediatric abdominal-pain presentation terminology and the need to assess for urgent causes.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Defines acute pediatric abdominal pain as a common nonspecific presentation requiring assessment for urgent causes and red flags.

source.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Abdominal pain — acute

examination.text: Specifies appearance and movement, vital signs, hydration, abdominal inspection, tenderness, guarding, rigidity, masses, and serial examination.
examination.rationale: Directly supports mapped appearance, vital signs, hydration, abdominal examination, tenderness, guarding, rigidity, and distension documentation.
examination.evidence_extract: Specifies appearance and movement, vital signs, hydration, abdominal inspection, tenderness, guarding, rigidity, masses, and serial examination.

source.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Abdominal pain — acute

investigations.text: States that most children need no investigations and lists targeted urine, blood, and imaging investigations selected by the likely cause.
investigations.rationale: Directly supports mapped urinalysis and clinician-selected investigation documentation while noting that most children need none.
investigations.evidence_extract: States that most children need no investigations and lists targeted urine, blood, and imaging investigations selected by the likely cause.

source.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Abdominal pain — acute

follow_up.text: Lists severe pain, peritonism, bilious vomiting, suspected surgical disease, admission, follow-up, and parent advice contexts.
follow_up.rationale: Directly supports mapped severe-pain, peritonism, bilious-vomiting, follow-up, and parent safety-net documentation.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Lists severe pain, peritonism, bilious vomiting, suspected surgical disease, admission, follow-up, and parent advice contexts.

source_limitations: No exact UAE pediatric abdominal-pain guideline was identified in the reasonable authoritative search.
Chronic, recurrent, functional, and constipation-labelled content is not fully covered by the acute source.
The selected source is hospital/emergency and Australia-specific rather than UAE outpatient guidance.
Dietary, hydration, laxative, and fixed follow-up content remains unsupported except for documented safety-net and follow-up decisions.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
